# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

# app/controllers/products_controller.rb
class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @products = Product.all
    @products = filter_products(@products)
    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 20)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  def search
    @products = Product.all
    @products = filter_products(@products)
    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 20)

    render :index
  end

  private

  def filter_products(scope)
    search_params = params.fetch(:search, {}).permit(
      :name,
      :category,
      :category_id,
      :min_price,
      :max_price,
      :sort_by,
      :sort_direction,
      :in_stock,
      :brand,
      tags: []
    )

    scope = filter_by_name(scope, search_params[:name])
    scope = filter_by_category(scope, search_params[:category], search_params[:category_id])
    scope = filter_by_price_range(scope, search_params[:min_price], search_params[:max_price])
    scope = filter_by_stock(scope, search_params[:in_stock])
    scope = filter_by_brand(scope, search_params[:brand])
    scope = filter_by_tags(scope, search_params[:tags])
    scope = apply_sorting(scope, search_params[:sort_by], search_params[:sort_direction])

    scope
  end

  def filter_by_name(scope, name)
    return scope if name.blank?

    # Secure parameterized query — NEVER use string interpolation like:
    #   where("name LIKE '%#{name}%'")  # ← SQL INJECTION VULNERABILITY
    scope.where("products.name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(name)}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(scope, category_name, category_id)
    if category_id.present?
      scope = scope.where(category_id: category_id)
    elsif category_name.present?
      scope = scope.joins(:category).where(categories: { name: category_name })
    end

    scope
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(scope, min_price, max_price)
    if min_price.present?
      min = BigDecimal(min_price.to_s)
      scope = scope.where("products.price >= ?", min)
    end

    if max_price.present?
      max = BigDecimal(max_price.to_s)
      scope = scope.where("products.price <= ?", max)
    end

    scope
  rescue ArgumentError
    scope
  end

  def filter_by_stock(scope, in_stock)
    return scope if in_stock.blank?

    if ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(in_stock)
      scope.where("products.stock_quantity > ?", 0)
    else
      scope.where(stock_quantity: 0)
    end
  end

  def filter_by_brand(scope, brand)
    return scope if brand.blank?

    scope.where(brand: brand)
  end

  def filter_by_tags(scope, tags)
    return scope if tags.blank?

    tags = Array(tags).reject(&:blank?)
    return scope if tags.empty?

    scope.joins(:tags).where(tags: { name: tags }).distinct
  end

  def apply_sorting(scope, sort_by, sort_direction)
    allowed_sort_columns = %w[name price created_at updated_at stock_quantity]
    allowed_directions = %w[asc desc]

    sort_by = allowed_sort_columns.include?(sort_by) ? sort_by : "created_at"
    sort_direction = allowed_directions.include?(sort_direction&.downcase) ? sort_direction.downcase : "desc"

    scope.order("products.#{sort_by} #{sort_direction}")
  end

  # Rails provides this helper to escape LIKE wildcards
  def sanitize_sql_like(string)
    ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(string)
  end
end
